advanced_machine_features.description: Specifies advanced machine features such as nested virtualization, UEFI networking, performance monitoring, and core/thread configurations. Optional parameters must align with the machine type and project settings.

allow_stopping_for_update.description: Indicates whether the instance can be stopped for updates during maintenance or configuration changes. Defaults to false if not specified.

attached_disk.description: Defines the properties of attached disks, including encryption settings, device name, mode (READ/WRITE), and source URL. Source is mandatory and must reference an existing disk.
attached_disk.type: list(object({device_name: optional(string),disk_encryption_key_raw: optional(string),disk_encryption_key_sha256: optional(string),kms_key_self_link: optional(string),mode: optional(string),source: string}))

boot_disk.description: Configuration for the instance's boot disk, including encryption, initialization parameters, and whether the disk is auto-deleted. Supports multiple initialization and encryption options.
boot_disk.type: list(object({auto_delete: optional(bool),device_name: optional(string),disk_encryption_key_raw: optional(string),disk_encryption_key_sha256: optional(string),interface: optional(string),kms_key_self_link: optional(string),mode: optional(string),source: optional(string),initialize_params: list(object({enable_confidential_compute: optional(bool),image: optional(string),labels: optional(map(string)),provisioned_iops: optional(number),provisioned_throughput: optional(number),resource_manager_tags: optional(map(string)),resource_policies: optional(list(string)),size: optional(number),storage_pool: optional(string),type: optional(string)}))}))

can_ip_forward.description: Determines whether the instance allows IP forwarding. This is required for certain networking configurations, such as creating gateways.

confidential_instance_config.description: Specifies settings for confidential instances, including enabling confidential compute and selecting the instance type. Only applicable to supported machine types.

deletion_protection.description: Prevents accidental deletion of the instance when enabled. Must be explicitly set to false to allow instance deletion.

description.description: An optional, human-readable description of the instance. Useful for documentation or identification purposes.

desired_status.description: Defines the intended state of the instance, such as RUNNING or TERMINATED. State transitions must comply with GCP instance lifecycle rules.

enable_display.description: Enables a display device on the instance, which is required for graphical applications. Typically used for Windows instances.

guest_accelerator.description: Specifies the type and number of GPUs to attach to the instance. GPU types must be supported in the selected zone and machine type.

hostname.description: Specifies a custom hostname for the instance. Must comply with DNS naming conventions if provided.

key_revocation_action_type.description: Defines the action to take when an encryption key is revoked, such as SHUTDOWN or DISABLE. Only applicable for instances with customer-managed encryption keys.

labels.description: A set of key-value pairs for organizing resources. Keys and values must be alphanumeric and adhere to GCP label guidelines.

machine_type.description: Specifies the type of machine to use for the instance, such as n1-standard-1. Must match the available machine types in the selected zone.

metadata.description: Key-value pairs of metadata to assign to the instance. Metadata can be used to configure the instance or pass data to startup scripts.

metadata_startup_script.description: A startup script to execute when the instance boots. The script must be a valid shell or batch script.

min_cpu_platform.description: Specifies the minimum CPU platform to use for the instance, such as Intel Skylake. Optional and only applicable for certain machine types.

name.description: The name of the instance. Must be unique within the project and follow GCP naming conventions.

network_interface.description: Configures network interfaces for the instance, including IP settings, access configurations, and network attachments. At least one interface must be defined.
network_interface.type: list(object({internal_ipv6_prefix_length: optional(number),ipv6_access_type: optional(string),ipv6_address: optional(string),name: optional(string),network: optional(string),network_attachment: optional(string),network_ip: optional(string),nic_type: optional(string),queue_count: optional(number),stack_type: optional(string),subnetwork: optional(string),subnetwork_project: optional(string),access_config: list(object({nat_ip: optional(string),network_tier: optional(string),public_ptr_domain_name: optional(string)})),alias_ip_range: list(object({ip_cidr_range: string,subnetwork_range_name: optional(string)})),ipv6_access_config: list(object({external_ipv6: optional(string),external_ipv6_prefix_length: optional(string),name: optional(string),network_tier: string,public_ptr_domain_name: optional(string)}))}))

network_performance_config.description: Specifies network performance settings, such as egress bandwidth tier. Only applicable for high-performance networking configurations.

params.description: Additional resource parameters, such as resource manager tags, for managing and organizing resources. Optional and must comply with GCP resource constraints.

reservation_affinity.description: Configures reservation affinity for the instance, specifying the type and any specific reservations to use. Reservations must exist in the project.

resource_policies.description: A list of resource policies to apply to the instance, such as snapshot schedules or maintenance windows. Policies must exist in the project.

scheduling.description: Specifies instance scheduling options, such as automatic restarts, preemptibility, and node affinities. Includes options for termination actions and maintenance behaviors.
scheduling.type: list(object({automatic_restart: optional(bool),availability_domain: optional(number),instance_termination_action: optional(string),min_node_cpus: optional(number),on_host_maintenance: optional(string),preemptible: optional(bool),provisioning_model: optional(string),termination_time: optional(string),local_ssd_recovery_timeout: list(object({nanos: optional(number),seconds: number})),max_run_duration: list(object({nanos: optional(number),seconds: number})),node_affinities: list(object({key: string,operator: string,values: list(string)})),on_instance_stop_action: list(object({discard_local_ssd: optional(bool)}))}))

scratch_disk.description: Defines scratch disks attached to the instance, including interface type and optional size. Scratch disks are ephemeral and ideal for temporary data.

service_account.description: Specifies the service account and scopes for the instance, which control access to GCP APIs. A default service account is used if not specified.

shielded_instance_config.description: Configures Shielded VM features for enhanced security, such as secure boot, vTPM, and integrity monitoring. Only applicable to Shielded VM-compatible machine types.

tags.description: A list of network tags to associate with the instance. Tags can be used for firewall rules and routing purposes.

timeouts.description: Specifies custom timeouts for instance creation, update, or deletion operations. Timeouts are defined as ISO 8601 duration strings.

zone.description: Defines the zone where the instance will be deployed. Must be a valid zone within the selected GCP region.
